channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Overflowing bins and fly-tipping causing obstruction and health hazard, 22nd April
item.description:
Large amounts of rubbish have been left beside and around the communal bins, which are visibly overflowing. Waste includes bin bags, cardboard, food packaging, and loose debris scattered across the ground. This is creating an obstruction on the pavement and poses a potential health and pest risk. The area appears not to have been cleared for some time. Please arrange urgent collection, clean-up, and consider increased bin capacity or more frequent collections to prevent recurrence. Outside Valois House , ( bin area ) Grange

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Grange, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

item.title: Bike blocking pavement, 22nd April
item.description:
Forest bike across pavement on Dressington Avenue,corner of Chudleigh Road (west side)
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chudleigh Road, Ladywell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Immediate Action Required: Dangerous Motorbike Use on Pedestrian Footpath, 22nd April
item.description:
Dear Council, I am writing to raise an urgent safety concern regarding a local footpath that is regularly being misused by motorbikes. On numerous occasions, I have witnessed motorbikes riding along the footpath and even using the adjacent grass area to bypass the stairs. This is not only inappropriate but extremely dangerous. The path is heavily used by pedestrians, including children travelling to and from Edgebury Primary School, making the risk of a serious accident very real. Alarmingly, my own child was nearly struck by a motorbike that approached at speed via the grass. This incident clearly demonstrates that the current situation is unsafe and cannot be allowed to continue. I urge the council to recognise the severity of this hazard and take immediate action to prevent motor vehicles from accessing the footpath. Installing a physical barrier at the entrance would be a straightforward and effective measure to protect the public. Please treat this matter with the urgency it deserves. I look forward to your prompt response outlining the steps that will be taken to address this danger. Photos attached. Thank you

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brownspring Drive, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Massively overgrown front garden, can we ask owner to fix?, 22nd April
item.description:
I live on this street and this owner rents this out, his tenants get no light into their house and it overall just looks rubbish and blocks the path. The tenants have themselves trimmed it back to help people access the pathway, i think the owner should get this sorted for the betterment of all the residents on this street including the tenants. Is there anything we can do to get in touch?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crewys Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Massively overgrown front garden, can we ask owner to fix?, 22nd April

item.title: Graffiti on a number of walls on the walkway between the underpass and Erith railway station., 22nd April
item.description:
Most of the walls have graffiti on them. This is the walkway between the underpass and Erith railway station. The problem is getting worse. Can it be removed, thanks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Victoria Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London

item.title: Shopping trolley full of black rubbish bags Victoria Road Erith, 22nd April
item.description:
Yet another shopping trolley has been left next to the yellow bin outside Christchurch in Victoria Road Erith. There is also litter in Victoria Road itself. Can these issues be resolved please.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Victoria Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress dumped on road, 22nd April
item.description:
Another idiot has dumped a massive mattress on the road right by where I live. I hope the person gets caught and fined, as there are CCTVs in the area

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Somerfield Road, Hackney, London

item.title: 55a, South End Road, Hampstead NW3 2QB, 22nd April
item.description:
The pavement lights outside unit 55a South End Road, Hampstead, are broken and letting rainwater into the basement unit which is currently a gents barber. It needs to be replaced and re sealed to avoid water damage to the basement shop unit going forward.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 57, South End Road, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
